Headline: Spaulding Block
Description: The Spaulding Block is a historic commercial building in Webster, Massachusetts.  One of the oldest commercial buildings in Webster, this three story brick building was built in 1866 by Cyrus Spaulding, and housed his hardware store.  The Webster Times was also an early occupant, after its previous home was burned out.  The building underwent a major renovation in 1897, receiving a new facade with Tudor styling.[2]

Headline: Upwey, Dorset
Description: Upwey is a suburb of Weymouth in south Dorset, England. The suburb is situated on the B3159 road in the Wey valley. The area was formerly a village until it was absorbed into the Weymouth built-up area. It is located four miles north of the town centre in the outer suburbs.  In the Census 2001 the combined population of Upwey and neighbouring Broadwey was 4,349.

Headline: Mapeley
Description: Mapeley Limited was a Guernsey-based outsourcing and property investment business. It was previously listed on the London Stock Exchange and constituent of the FTSE 250 Index.

Headline: Alain van Katwijk
Description: Alain van Katwijk (born 28 February 1979) is a Dutch former cyclist. His father Jan, as well as his uncles Piet and Fons, were also professional cyclists.[1]

Headline: D.King
Description: Justin Essandoh (born November 27, 1983), also known as D.King or DK, is an African rapper, songwriter, and recording artist in the African rap genre.  The name "D.King" or "DK" is short for "Don and the King".
